---
title: "phase1"
output: pdf_document
date: "2026-05-05"
---
```{r}
# install.packages("readxl")
# install.packages("ggplot2")
# install.packages("dplyr")
# install.packages("car")
library(readxl)
library(ggplot2)
library(dplyr)
library(car)
```

```{r}
# Set working directory if needed
# setwd("your/path/here")

# Read Excel file
temp <- read.csv("temp.csv")
ph <- read.csv("ph.csv")
temp_data <- temp[0:4]
ph_data <- ph[0:4]

# Ensure 'trial' and the treatment variables are factors for ANOVA
temp_data$Trial <- as.numeric(temp_data$Trial)
temp_data$Temp  <- as.numeric(temp_data$Temp)

ph_data$Trial <- as.numeric(ph_data$Trial)
ph_data$pH    <- as.numeric(ph_data$pH)

names(temp_data)[names(temp_data) == "Diameter..cm."] <- "Diameter"
names(ph_data)[names(ph_data) == "Diameter..cm."] <- "Diameter"

head(temp_data)
head(ph_data)
```
```{r}
ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = Temp, y = Diameter)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(method = "lm", formula = y ~ x, se = TRUE) +
  labs(
    title = "Linear Relationship between Temperature and Diameter",
    x = "Temperature",
    y = "Diameter (cm)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = Temp, y = Diameter)) +
  geom_point(alpha = 0.6) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    se = TRUE
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "Quadratic Relationship between Temperature and Diameter",
    x = "Temperature",
    y = "Diameter (cm)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

library(ggplot2)

ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = Temp, y = Diameter)) +

  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +

  # Linear
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ x,
    aes(linetype = "Linear"),
    se = FALSE
  ) +

  # Quadratic
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    aes(linetype = "Quadratic"),
    se = FALSE
  ) +

  # Cubic
  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ poly(x, 3),
    aes(linetype = "Cubic"),
    se = FALSE
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "Temperature Effect on Fungal Growth",
    x = "Temperature",
    y = "Diameter (cm)",
    linetype = "Model"
  ) +

  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = Temp, y = Diameter)) +
  geom_point(color = "grey30", size = 2.5, alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_smooth(
    method = "loess",
    color = "darkblue",
    fill = "lightblue",
    linewidth = 1.5
  ) +
  labs(
    title = "LOESS Trend for Temperature",
    x = "Temperature",
    y = "Diameter (cm)"
  ) +
  theme_bw()
```
```{r}

ggplot(ph_data, aes(x = pH, y = Diameter)) +

  geom_point(size = 2, alpha = 0.7) +

  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ x,
    aes(linetype = "Linear"),
    se = FALSE
  ) +

  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ poly(x, 2),
    aes(linetype = "Quadratic"),
    se = FALSE
  ) +

  geom_smooth(
    method = "lm",
    formula = y ~ poly(x, 3),
    aes(linetype = "Cubic"),
    se = FALSE
  ) +

  labs(
    title = "pH Effect on Fungal Growth",
    x = "pH",
    y = "Diameter (cm)",
    linetype = "Model"
  ) +

  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
# For Temperature Data: Clear Mean + 95% CI Plot broken down by Trial panels
ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = Temp, y = Diameter, color = Temp)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_normal, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, size = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5, shape = 18) +
  facet_grid(. ~ Trial, labeller = label_both) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Fungal Growth (95% CI) Across Temperature Treatments",
    subtitle = "Separated by Experimental Trial to view distribution consistency",
    x = "Temperature Level",
    y = "Colony Diameter (cm)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none") # Legend hidden because x-axis already displays temperature labels

# For pH Data: Clear Mean + 95% CI Plot broken down by Trial panels
ggplot(ph_data, aes(x = pH, y = Diameter, color = pH)) +
  stat_summary(fun.data = mean_cl_normal, geom = "errorbar", width = 0.2, size = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3.5, shape = 18) +
  facet_grid(. ~ Trial, labeller = label_both) +
  labs(
    title = "Mean Fungal Growth (95% CI) Across pH Treatments",
    subtitle = "Separated by Experimental Trial to highlight interaction changes",
    x = "pH Level",
    y = "Colony Diameter (cm)"
  ) +
  theme_bw() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```
```{r}
# Temperature Interaction Plot
ggplot(temp_data, aes(x = as.numeric(as.character(Temp)), y = Diameter, color = Trial, group = Trial)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Interaction Plot: Mean Fungal Growth across Temperatures",
       x = "Temperature",
       y = "Mean Diameter (cm)") +
  theme_classic()

# pH Interaction Plot
ggplot(ph_data, aes(x = as.numeric(as.character(pH)), y = Diameter, color = Trial, group = Trial)) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "line", size = 1) +
  stat_summary(fun = mean, geom = "point", size = 3) +
  labs(title = "Interaction Plot: Mean Fungal Growth across pH Levels",
       x = "pH Level",
       y = "Mean Diameter (cm)") +
  theme_classic()
```



# Temp model
```{r}
library(dplyr)

# Filter for rows where Diameter is NOT equal to 0
temp_data <- temp_data %>% filter(Diameter != 0)
ph_data   <- ph_data   %>% filter(Diameter != 0)
```


```{r temp-model}
# 2. Fit basic quadratic model (No Trial factor included)
temp_model <- lm(Diameter ~ (Temp + I(Temp^2) + I(Temp^3)) * Trial, data = temp_data)

# 3. Print evaluation outputs
cat("\n--- Temperature Model Summary ---\n")
print(summary(temp_model))

cat("\n--- ANOVA Table for Temperature Regression ---\n")
print(anova(temp_model))

temp_model_final <- lm(Diameter ~ (Temp + I(Temp^2) + I(Temp^3)), data = temp_data)


# 4. Check assumptions
plot(temp_model_final, which = 1, main = "Residuals vs Fitted: Temperature Model")
plot(temp_model_final, which = 2, main = "Normal Q-Q: Temperature Model")

```
```{r}
cat("\n--- Temperature Model Summary ---\n")
print(summary(temp_model_final))

cat("\n--- ANOVA Table for Temperature Regression ---\n")
print(anova(temp_model_final))
```

```{r}
# 1. Scale-Location Plot (Homoscedasticity)
# Look for a flat horizontal red line with random point spread
plot(temp_model_final, which = 3, main = "Scale-Location: Temperature Model")

# 2. Cook's Distance (Influential Points)
# Points 11, 13, and 37 appeared in your other plots; 
# check if they exceed the dotted red threshold here.
plot(temp_model_final, which = 4, main = "Cook's Distance: Temperature Model")

# 3. Durbin-Watson Test (Independence)
# Requires the 'car' package
# A result around 2.0 suggests independence.
if(!require(car)) install.packages("car")
library(car)
durbinWatsonTest(temp_model_final)

# 4. Shapiro-Wilk Test (Formal Normality Check)
# A p-value > 0.05 means your residuals are normally distributed.
shapiro.test(residuals(temp_model_final))
```




# ph model


```{r ph-model}

# 2. Fit specific model: Linear interaction with Trial + Global Quadratic term
ph_model <- lm(Diameter ~ (pH + I(pH^2)) * Trial, data = ph_data)

# 3. Print evaluation outputs
cat("\n--- pH Model Summary ---\n")
print(summary(ph_model))

cat("\n--- ANOVA Table for pH Regression ---\n")
print(anova(ph_model))

# 4. Check assumptions
plot(ph_model, which = 1, main = "Residuals vs Fitted: pH Model")
plot(ph_model, which = 2, main = "Normal Q-Q: pH Model")
```
```{r}
plot(ph_model, which = 3, main = "Scale-Location: pH Level Model")

# 2. Cook's Distance (Influential Points)
# Points 11, 13, and 37 appeared in your other plots; 
# check if they exceed the dotted red threshold here.
plot(ph_model, which = 4, main = "Cook's Distance: pH Level Model")

# 3. Durbin-Watson Test (Independence)
# Requires the 'car' package
# A result around 2.0 suggests independence.
if(!require(car)) install.packages("car")
library(car)
durbinWatsonTest(ph_model)

# 4. Shapiro-Wilk Test (Formal Normality Check)
# A p-value > 0.05 means your residuals are normally distributed.
shapiro.test(residuals(ph_model))
```


```{r}
ph_model_final <- lm(Diameter ~ (pH) * Trial + I(pH^2), data = ph_data)
```

```{r}
cat("\n--- Temperature Model Summary ---\n")
print(summary(ph_model_final))

cat("\n--- ANOVA Table for Temperature Regression ---\n")
print(anova(ph_model_final))
```
